ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "movie_name" TEXT /* Name of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the movie was released */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the movie */,
  "director" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "producer" TEXT /* Name of the producer */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget for the movie production */,
  "marketing_budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for marketing the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Length of the movie in minutes */,
  "distributor" TEXT /* Company distributing the movie */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Company producing the movie */,
  "cast" TEXT /* Key cast members of the movie */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of production */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the movie */,
  "mpaa_rating" TEXT /* MPAA rating */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Brief summary of the movie plot */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie trailer */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie poster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "box_office_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "opening_day_earnings" REAL /* Earnings on the opening day */,
  "opening_weekend_earnings" TEXT /* Earnings during the opening weekend */,
  "end_of_week_1_earnings" TEXT /* Earnings at the end of the first week */,
  "lifetime_earnings" REAL /* Total lifetime earnings of the movie */,
  "domestic_opening_day_earnings" REAL /* Earnings on the opening day in the domestic market */,
  "international_opening_day_earnings" REAL /* Earnings on the opening day in the international market */,
  "domestic_opening_weekend_earnings" REAL /* Earnings during the opening weekend in the domestic market */,
  "international_opening_weekend_earnings" REAL /* Earnings during the opening weekend in the international market */,
  "domestic_lifetime_earnings" REAL /* Total lifetime earnings in the domestic market */,
  "international_lifetime_earnings" REAL /* Total lifetime earnings in the international market */,
  "end_of_week_2_earnings" REAL /* Earnings at the end of the second week */,
  "end_of_week_3_earnings" REAL /* Earnings at the end of the third week */,
  "end_of_week_4_earnings" REAL /* Earnings at the end of the fourth week */,
  "screens" INTEGER /* Number of screens the movie was shown on */,
  "theaters" INTEGER /* Number of theaters the movie was shown in */,
  "weekend_rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the movie on the opening weekend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_box_office_performance_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "channels" TEXT /* Channels used for the campaign (e.g., TV, Social Media, Print) */,
  "total_spend" REAL /* Total amount spent on the campaign */,
  "campaign_objective" TEXT /* Primary objective of the campaign */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Demographic target of the campaign */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions generated by the campaign */,
  "clicks" INTEGER /* Number of clicks on campaign ads */,
  "conversion_rate" REAL /* Conversion rate of the campaign */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audience_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "social_media_followers" INTEGER /* Number of social media followers */,
  "trailers_views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the movie trailers */,
  "premieres_attendance" INTEGER /* Number of attendees at the premiere */,
  "ratings" REAL /* Average rating of the movie */,
  "reviews" INTEGER /* Number of reviews for the movie */,
  "social_media_engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate on social media platforms */,
  "trailer_engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate on trailer views */,
  "premiere_engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate at the premiere */,
  "positive_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of positive reviews */,
  "negative_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of negative reviews */,
  "neutral_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of neutral reviews */,
  "social_media_mentions" INTEGER /* Number of mentions on social media */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Overall sentiment score of the audience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audience_engagement_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., executive, marketing team, analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions granted to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the platform was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, report) */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie accessed (if applicable) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the platform */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in minutes */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the user during access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
